title: Missing Data Imputation
text:
6 Visualize Imputation Diagnostics


Code
# function to create density comparison plots for imputation diagnostics
plot_imputation_diagnostic <- function(orig_data, imputed_data, variable) {
  x_orig <- orig_data[[variable]]
  x_imp <- imputed_data[[variable]]

  miss_idx <- is.na(x_orig)
  n_miss <- sum(miss_idx)
  n_tot <- length(x_orig)

  # nothing to diagnose if no missingness
  if (n_miss == 0) {
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # caption text showing missing rate
  caption_txt <- sprintf("Missing: %d (%.1f%%)", n_miss, 100 * n_miss / n_tot)

  # factor/character path: bar chart of proportions
  if (is.factor(x_orig) || is.character(x_orig)) {
    x_orig_f <- as.factor(x_orig)
    x_imp_f <- as.factor(x_imp)

    # observed distribution (excluding NAs)
    obs_tab <- prop.table(table(x_orig_f[!is.na(x_orig_f)]))

    # imputed-at-missing distribution
    imp_tab <- prop.table(table(x_imp_f[miss_idx]))

    levs <- union(names(obs_tab), names(imp_tab))
    df_plot <- data.frame(
      level = levs,
      observed = as.numeric(obs_tab[levs]),
      imputed_missing = as.numeric(imp_tab[levs])
    )
    df_plot[is.na(df_plot)] <- 0

    df_long <- df_plot %>%
      tidyr::pivot_longer(
        cols = c("observed", "imputed_missing"),
        names_to = "source",
        values_to = "prop"
      ) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        source = factor(
          source,
          levels = c("observed", "imputed_missing"),
          labels = c("Observed (non-missing)", "Imputed (missing only)")
        )
      )

    print(
      ggplot2::ggplot(df_long, ggplot2::aes(x = level, y = prop, fill = source)) +
        ggplot2::geom_col(position = "dodge") +
        ggplot2::labs(
          title = gsub("<.*?>", "", beautify_names(variable)),
          x = gsub("<.*?>", "", beautify_names(variable)),
          y = "Proportion",
          fill = NULL,
          caption = caption_txt
        ) +
        ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    )

    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # numeric path: density comparison
  x_orig_num <- as.numeric(x_orig)
  x_obs <- x_orig_num[!miss_idx]
  x_imp_miss <- as.numeric(x_imp)[miss_idx]

  # keep only finite values
  x_obs <- x_obs[is.finite(x_obs)]
  x_imp_miss <- x_imp_miss[is.finite(x_imp_miss)]

  # if too few points for density, fall back to point plot
  if (length(x_imp_miss) < 2L || length(x_obs) < 2L) {
    df_fallback <- data.frame(
      value = c(x_obs, x_imp_miss),
      source = c(
        rep("Observed (non-missing)", length(x_obs)),
        rep("Imputed (missing only)", length(x_imp_miss))
      )
    )

    print(
      ggplot2::ggplot(df_fallback, ggplot2::aes(x = value, y = source)) +
        ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
        ggplot2::labs(
          title = gsub("<.*?>", "", beautify_names(variable)),
          x = gsub("<.*?>", "", beautify_names(variable)),
          y = NULL,
          caption = paste0(caption_txt, " | density skipped (too few points)")
        )
    )

    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # compute kernel density estimates
  dens_obs <- stats::density(x_obs)
  dens_imp <- stats::density(x_imp_miss)

  df_dens <- data.frame(
    x = dens_obs$x,
    observed = dens_obs$y,
    imputed_missing = stats::approx(
      x = dens_imp$x, y = dens_imp$y,
      xout = dens_obs$x, rule = 2
    )$y
  )

  df_long <- df_dens %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c("observed", "imputed_missing"),
      names_to = "source",
      values_to = "density"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      source = factor(
        source,
        levels = c("observed", "imputed_missing"),
        labels = c("Observed (non-missing)", "Imputed (missing only)")
      )
    )

  print(
    ggplot2::ggplot(df_long, ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = density, color = source)) +
      ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
      ggplot2::labs(
        title = gsub("<.*?>", "", beautify_names(variable)),
        x = gsub("<.*?>", "", beautify_names(variable)),
        y = "Density",
        color = NULL,
        caption = caption_txt
      )
  )

  invisible(NULL)
}




Code
# identify variables with missing values for the diagnostic figures
vars_with_missing <- missing_info$var[missing_info$missing_rate > 0]



Code
# generate diagnostic plots for each variable with missing values
vars_with_missing %>%
  purrr::walk(~ plot_imputation_diagnostic(
    orig_data = df_impute,
    imputed_data = imp_mf$ximp,
    variable = .x
  ))







































Observed versus Imputed Distributions (imputed values shown only at originally missing cells)

title: Missing Data Imputation
text:
7 Compare Descriptive Statistics


Code
# compute descriptive statistics for observed and imputed numeric data
summ_obs <- df_impute %>%
  numeric_df() %>%
  describe(print_only = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::rename(variable = var) %>%
  dplyr::select(-n)

summ_imp <- imp_mf$ximp %>%
  numeric_df() %>%
  describe(print_only = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::rename(variable = var) %>%
  dplyr::select(-n)

comparison <- versus::compare(
  summ_obs, summ_imp,
  by = variable
) %>%
  versus::value_diffs_stacked(column = -dplyr::any_of("p")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(diff = val_b - val_a, .after = "val_b") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(variable, column)

# format column names for display via literal lookup (regex replacement
# consumes backslashes in stringr/ICU replacement strings)
metric_labels <- c(
  "mean" = "$\\bar{x}$", "sd" = "$\\sigma$",
  "skew" = "$Sk$", "se" = "$SE$", "kurtosis" = "$K$",
  "norm" = "Normality"
)
comparison$column <- md_to_html(
  dplyr::coalesce(unname(metric_labels[comparison$column]), comparison$column)
)




Code
# table metadata for comparison display
footnote_text <- glue(
  "$N$ = {nrow(df_impute)}; Descriptive statistics compared between observed ",
  "values (missing ignored) and the fully imputed dataset."
)
col_names <- c("Metric", "Observed", "Imputed", "Difference")




Code
# prepare row grouping for table display
pack_row_indices <- row_grouping(comparison$variable)
names(pack_row_indices) %<>% beautify_names()

# display the comparison table with grouped rows
comparison %>%
  dplyr::select(-variable) %>%

title: Missing Data Imputation
text:
8 Integrate Final Dataset

in: imp_mf$ximp (imputed partnered cases), df_non_partnered (held out)
out: imputed_data_combined.sav
rule: rejoin imputed partnered with held-out non-partnered; preserve structural NAs on partner variables for non-partnered cases



Code
# verify complete cases remain unchanged post-imputation
complete_idx <- which(complete.cases(df_impute))
stopifnot(identical(df_impute[complete_idx, ], imp_mf$ximp[complete_idx, ]))

# add identifier, partner status, and passthrough columns back to imputed data
imp_mf$ximp$idnummer <- df_partnered$idnummer
imp_mf$ximp$partner <- 1
imp_mf$ximp <- dplyr::left_join(
  imp_mf$ximp,
  df_partnered %>%
    dplyr::select(idnummer, dplyr::all_of(passthrough_vars)),
  by = "idnummer"
)

# combine imputed partnered data with non-partnered data
imputed_data_combined <- copy_labels(
  source = df_with_missings,
  target = imp_mf$ximp
) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows(df_non_partnered) %>%
  dplyr::select(idnummer, dplyr::everything())

# save the final combined dataset
haven::write_sav(
  imputed_data_combined,
  here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "imputed_data_combined.sav")
)




Code
# generate summary of imputation process
htmltools::HTML(glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
  <strong>Imputation Complete</strong><br><br>
  <strong>Partnered respondents imputed:</strong> {nrow(df_partnered)}<br>
  <strong>Non-partnered respondents (held out):</strong> {nrow(df_non_partnered)}<br>
  <strong>Combined dataset:</strong> {nrow(imputed_data_combined)} observations<br>
  <strong>Variables with imputed values:</strong> {length(vars_with_missing)}<br><br>
  <strong>Output:</strong> <code>imputed_data_combined.sav</code> for Step 6
  </div>"
))

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
Code
# clear global environment, garbage collect, and detach packages
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "robust_analysis", "standardize", "create_lmrob_control", "save_function",
  "named_list", "numeric_df", "set_globals", "import_pipes", "process_model",
  "fit_robust_model", "sci_to_tex", "prepare_model_matrices", "fit_models"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

# global recompute switch
# TRUE re-runs the heavy steps; FALSE loads cached assets when they exist
recompute_heavy <- FALSE

# define analysis type
type <- "unadjusted"
options(width = 250)

# load analysis-ready dataset from step 6
analysis_data <- haven::read_sav(
  here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "analysis_ready.sav")
)

models_dir <- here::here("assets", "models")
if (!dir.exists(models_dir)) dir.create(models_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# consolidated output path; cached fits are reused when this file exists
consolidated_path <- file.path(models_dir, paste0(type, "_fits.rds"))

# refit when forced or when no cached consolidated fits exist
refit_models <- recompute_heavy || !file.exists(consolidated_path)

# when refitting, purge only this step's outputs (stale fits and specs);
# step 9 fits and step 10 caches stay intact
if (refit_models) {
  unlink(Sys.glob(file.path(models_dir, "unadjusted_*")))
  unlink(file.path(models_dir, "models.rds"))
}

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
1 Verify Dataset from Step 6
Confirm expected variables are present before model fitting.


Code
# define expected variables from step 6
ses_composites <- c("education_parental", "isei08_parental")

# partner SES (structural NA for non-partnered)
partner_ses <- c("education_partner", "isei08_partner")

# individual parent variables for disaggregated models
parental_disagg <- c(
  "education_father", "education_mother",
  "isei08_father", "isei08_mother"
)

# core regression variables
core_vars <- c(
  "dhd_index", "woman", "age", "partner",
  "education_self", "isei08_self"
)

# optional variables (may or may not be present)
optional_vars <- c("dhd_kcal", "smoking", "income_hh")

# check presence
ses_present <- ses_composites[ses_composites %in% names(analysis_data)]
ses_missing <- setdiff(ses_composites, ses_present)
partner_present <- partner_ses[partner_ses %in% names(analysis_data)]
partner_missing <- setdiff(partner_ses, partner_present)
parental_present <- parental_disagg[parental_disagg %in% names(analysis_data)]
parental_missing <- setdiff(parental_disagg, parental_present)
core_present <- core_vars[core_vars %in% names(analysis_data)]
core_missing <- setdiff(core_vars, core_present)
optional_present <- optional_vars[optional_vars %in% names(analysis_data)]

# sample sizes
n_total <- nrow(analysis_data)
n_partnered <- sum(analysis_data$partner == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
n_non_partnered <- sum(analysis_data$partner == 0, na.rm = TRUE)

# display verification results
verify_msg <- glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
<strong>Dataset Verification (Step 6)</strong><br><br>
<strong>Input:</strong> {n_total} observations<br>
<strong>Partnered:</strong> {n_partnered} ({round(100 * n_partnered / n_total, 1)}%)<br>
<strong>Non-partnered:</strong> {n_non_partnered} ({round(100 * n_non_partnered / n_total, 1)}%)<br><br>
<strong>Core variables:</strong> {if (length(core_missing) == 0) 'All present ✓' else paste('Missing:', paste(core_missing, collapse = ', '))}<br>
<strong>Parental composites:</strong> {if (length(ses_missing) == 0) 'All present ✓' else paste('Missing:', paste(ses_missing, collapse = ', '))}<br>
<strong>Parental disaggregated:</strong> {if (length(parental_missing) == 0) 'All present ✓' else paste('Missing:', paste(parental_missing, collapse = ', '))}<br>
<strong>Partner SES:</strong> {if (length(partner_missing) == 0) 'All present ✓' else paste('Missing:', paste(partner_missing, collapse = ', '))}<br>
<strong>Optional variables:</strong> {if (length(optional_present) > 0) paste(optional_present, collapse = ', ') else 'None'}
</div>"
)
cat(verify_msg)







Note



Dataset Verification (Step 6) Input: 922 observations Partnered: 771 (83.6%) Non-partnered: 151 (16.4%) Core variables: All present ✓ Parental composites: All present ✓ Parental disaggregated: All present ✓ Partner SES: All present ✓ Optional variables: dhd_kcal, smoking, income_hh



Code
# stop if critical variables are missing
if (length(core_missing) > 0) {
  stop("Missing core variables: ", paste(core_missing, collapse = ", "))
}
if (length(ses_missing) > 0) {
  warning("Missing parental composites: ", paste(ses_missing, collapse = ", "))
}

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
2 Model Specifications

2.1 Model 0: Null Model


Code
model_0 <- dhd_index ~ 1




2.2 Model 1: Adult Covariates and Own SES (Adjustment Set)


Code
# base model: demographics + lifestyle + own SES
base_formula <- dhd_index ~ woman + age + partner +
  education_self + isei08_self

# nutritional-epidemiology covariate: total energy intake
if ("dhd_kcal" %in% names(analysis_data)) {
  base_formula <- update(base_formula, . ~ . + dhd_kcal)
}
# lifestyle covariate: smoking status
if ("smoking" %in% names(analysis_data)) {
  base_formula <- update(base_formula, . ~ . + smoking)
}
# household economics (optional, conditional on availability)
if ("income_hh" %in% names(analysis_data)) {
  base_formula <- update(base_formula, . ~ . + income_hh)
}

model_1 <- base_formula

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
3 Composite SES Models (Models 2–6)
These models use parental composites that average mother and father within education and occupation domains.

3.1 Model 2: Add Parental SES Main Effects (H1, RQ1)


Code
model_2 <- update(model_1, . ~ . +
  education_parental +
  isei08_parental)




3.2 Model 3: Add Partner SES Main Effects (H2, RQ2)


Code
model_3 <- update(model_2, . ~ . +
  education_partner:partner +
  isei08_partner:partner)




3.3 Model 4: Gender Moderation of Parental and Partner SES (H3, H4, RQ3)


Code
model_4 <- update(model_3, . ~ . +
  woman:education_parental +
  woman:isei08_parental +
  woman:education_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_partner:partner)




3.4 Model 5: Parental × Partner SES Synergy (H5, RQ4)


Code
model_5 <- update(model_4, . ~ . +
  education_parental:education_partner:partner +
  isei08_parental:isei08_partner:partner)




3.5 Model 6: Gender Moderation of Synergy (H6, RQ4)


Code
model_6 <- update(model_5, . ~ . +
  woman:education_parental:education_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_parental:isei08_partner:partner)

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
4 Disaggregated SES Models (Models 7–12)
These models use separate variables for mother, father, and partner education and occupation.

4.1 Model 7: Mother and Father SES Main Effects (H1, RQ1)


Code
model_7 <- update(model_1, . ~ . +
  education_mother +
  education_father +
  isei08_mother +
  isei08_father)




4.2 Model 8: Add Partner SES Main Effects (H2, RQ2)


Code
model_8 <- update(model_7, . ~ . +
  education_partner:partner +
  isei08_partner:partner)




4.3 Model 9: Gender × Mother/Father/Partner SES Interactions (H3, H4, RQ3)


Code
model_9 <- update(model_8, . ~ . +
  woman:education_mother +
  woman:education_father +
  woman:isei08_mother +
  woman:isei08_father +
  woman:education_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_partner:partner)




4.4 Model 10: Mother × Partner and Father × Partner Synergy (H5, RQ4)


Code
model_10 <- update(model_9, . ~ . +
  education_mother:education_partner:partner +
  education_father:education_partner:partner +
  isei08_mother:isei08_partner:partner +
  isei08_father:isei08_partner:partner)




4.5 Model 11: Gender × Parent-Specific Synergy (H6, RQ4)


Code
model_11 <- update(model_10, . ~ . +
  woman:education_mother:education_partner:partner +
  woman:education_father:education_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_mother:isei08_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_father:isei08_partner:partner)




4.6 Model 12: Full Disaggregated Model (Complete Specification)


Code
# model 12 is equivalent to model 11
model_12 <- model_11

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
5 Prepare Data for Model Fitting


Code
# collect model objects in numeric order
model_names <- ls(pattern = "^model_[0-9]+$")
model_names <- model_names[order(as.integer(sub("model_", "", model_names)))]
models <- lapply(model_names, function(nm) get(nm, envir = .GlobalEnv))
names(models) <- model_names

# save model specifications
saveRDS(models, file = file.path(models_dir, "models.rds"))

# prepare analysis data
unadjusted_data <- numeric_df(analysis_data) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # ensure partner is numeric binary
    partner = as.numeric(partner == 1),

    # condition partner SES on having a partner (set to 0 for non-partnered)
    education_partner = dplyr::if_else(partner == 0, 0, education_partner),
    isei08_partner = dplyr::if_else(partner == 0, 0, isei08_partner)
  )

# identify variables used in models
vars_in_models <- unique(unlist(lapply(models, function(m) {
  as.character(attr(stats::terms(m), "variables"))[-1]
})))

# define analysis variable set
analysis_vars <- c(
  "dhd_index",
  # parental composites
  "education_parental", "isei08_parental",
  # disaggregated parental
  "education_father", "education_mother",
  "isei08_father", "isei08_mother",
  # partner
  "education_partner", "isei08_partner",
  # own SES
  "education_self", "isei08_self",
  # demographics
  "partner", "woman", "age"
)

# add optional covariates if present
if ("dhd_kcal" %in% names(unadjusted_data)) {
  analysis_vars <- c(analysis_vars, "dhd_kcal")
}
if ("smoking" %in% names(unadjusted_data)) {
  analysis_vars <- c(analysis_vars, "smoking")
}
if ("income_hh" %in% names(unadjusted_data)) {
  analysis_vars <- c(analysis_vars, "income_hh")
}

vars_keep <- unique(c(vars_in_models, analysis_vars))
vars_keep <- intersect(vars_keep, names(unadjusted_data))

unadjusted_data <- unadjusted_data[vars_keep]

# save preprocessed dataset
saveRDS(unadjusted_data, file = here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "unadjusted_data.rds"))

# disable bootstrapping for baseline fits
n_bootstraps <- 0




Code
data_msg <- glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
<strong>Analysis Dataset Prepared</strong><br><br>
<strong>Observations:</strong> {nrow(unadjusted_data)}<br>
<strong>Variables retained:</strong> {ncol(unadjusted_data)}<br>
<strong>Models specified:</strong> {length(models)} (Models 0-{length(models) - 1})<br>
<strong>Bootstrap iterations:</strong> {n_bootstraps} (disabled for baseline)<br><br>
<strong>Parental composites:</strong> <code>education_parental</code>, <code>isei08_parental</code><br>
<strong>Disaggregated parental:</strong> <code>education_mother</code>, <code>education_father</code>, <code>isei08_mother</code>, <code>isei08_father</code><br>
<strong>Partner SES:</strong> <code>education_partner</code>, <code>isei08_partner</code>
</div>"
)
cat(data_msg)







Note



Analysis Dataset Prepared Observations: 922 Variables retained: 17 Models specified: 12 (Models 0-11) Bootstrap iterations: 0 (disabled for baseline) Parental composites: education_parental, isei08_parental Disaggregated parental: education_mother, education_father, isei08_mother, isei08_father Partner SES: education_partner, isei08_partner

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
6 Fit Regression Models

6.1 Fit Model 0: Null Model (Baseline)


Code
fit_0 <- robust_analysis(
  data = get(glue("{type}_data")),
  form = model_0,
  n_boots = n_bootstraps,
  pct = 0.95,

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
Influence Diagnostics & Sensitivity Sample
text:
Code
# clear global environment, garbage collect, and detach packages
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions (consistent with step 7)
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "as_kable", "set_globals", "pval_str", "add_style", "sci_to_tex",
  "md_to_html", "describe", "format_p_value", "std_res", "footnote",
  "beautify_names", "min_bootstraps", "numeric_df", "import_pipes"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()
Code
# define input paths from step 7
unadjusted_data_path <- here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "unadjusted_data.rds")
unadjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "unadjusted_fits.rds")

# validate input files exist
if (!file.exists(unadjusted_data_path)) {
  stop("Missing input from Step 7: ", unadjusted_data_path)
}
if (!file.exists(unadjusted_fits_path)) {
  stop("Missing input from Step 7: ", unadjusted_fits_path)
}

# load unadjusted dataset
unadjusted_data <- readRDS(unadjusted_data_path)
if (!is.data.frame(unadjusted_data)) {
  stop("'unadjusted_data' must be a data.frame.")
}

# retrieve robust regression models from step 7
# step 7's robust_analysis() returns structured_models objects:
# - $robust_model_results$model_object for lmrob fits
# - $ols_model_results$model_object for OLS fits
fits_raw <- readRDS(unadjusted_fits_path)
if (!is.list(fits_raw) || length(fits_raw) < 2) {
  stop("Expected a list of model fits in ", unadjusted_fits_path)
}

# extract lmrob fits, dropping null model (fit_0)
lmrob_fits <- fits_raw %>%
  purrr::map(~ .x$robust_model_results$model_object) %>%
  tail(-1)

if (length(lmrob_fits) == 0) {
  stop("No models found after dropping the null model.")
}
if (is.null(names(lmrob_fits)) || any(names(lmrob_fits) == "")) {
  names(lmrob_fits) <- paste0("fit_", seq_along(lmrob_fits))
}

# extract OLS companion fits (pre-computed in step 7)
ols_fits <- fits_raw %>%
  purrr::map(~ .x$ols_model_results$model_object) %>%
  tail(-1)
names(ols_fits) <- names(lmrob_fits)

# record model count for downstream use
n_models <- length(lmrob_fits)
cat(glue("Loaded {n_models} substantive models (Models 1-{n_models}) from Step 7.\n\n"))


Loaded 11 substantive models (Models 1-11) from Step 7.
Code
# display verification summary
verify_msg <- glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
<strong>Step 7 Alignment Verification</strong><br><br>
<strong>Data source:</strong> <code>unadjusted_data.rds</code> ({nrow(unadjusted_data)} observations, {ncol(unadjusted_data)} variables)<br>
<strong>Model fits:</strong> <code>unadjusted_fits.rds</code> ({n_models} substantive models)<br>
<strong>Model names:</strong> {paste(names(lmrob_fits), collapse = ', ')}<br>
<strong>OLS companions:</strong> Pre-computed in Step 7 (not rebuilt)
</div>"
)
cat(verify_msg)







Note



Step 7 Alignment Verification Data source: unadjusted_data.rds (922 observations, 17 variables) Model fits: unadjusted_fits.rds (11 substantive models) Model names: fit_1, fit_2, fit_3, fit_4, fit_5, fit_6, fit_7, fit_8, fit_9, fit_10, fit_11 OLS companions: Pre-computed in Step 7 (not rebuilt)

title: Influence Diagnostics & Sensitivity Sample
text:
1 Diagnostic Tests and Assumption Checks

1.1 Assessing Residual Shape


Code
# compute sample skewness and kurtosis without extra packages
skew_kurt <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n < 4) {
    return(c(skewness = NA_real_, kurtosis = NA_real_))
  }

  m <- mean(x)
  s <- stats::sd(x)
  if (!is.finite(s) || s <= .Machine$double.eps) {
    return(c(skewness = NA_real_, kurtosis = NA_real_))
  }

  z <- (x - m) / s
  c(
    skewness = mean(z^3),
    kurtosis = mean(z^4)
  )
}




Code
# summarize standardized robust residuals for a single model
residual_shape <- function(fit) {
  r <- stats::residuals(fit) / fit$scale
  r <- r[is.finite(r)]

  if (length(r) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      n = 0L,
      mean = NA_real_,
      sd = NA_real_,
      median = NA_real_,
      MAD = NA_real_,
      skewness = NA_real_,
      kurtosis = NA_real_,
      p_gt2 = NA_real_,
      p_gt3 = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  sk <- skew_kurt(r)

  data.frame(
    n = length(r),
    mean = mean(r),
    sd = stats::sd(r),
    median = stats::median(r),
    MAD = stats::mad(r),
    skewness = unname(sk["skewness"]),
    kurtosis = unname(sk["kurtosis"]),
    p_gt2 = mean(abs(r) > 2),
    p_gt3 = mean(abs(r) > 3)
  )
}




Code
# table metadata for residual shape summary
col_names <- c(
  "Model", "$n$", "mean", "sd", "median", "MAD",
  "skewness", "kurtosis", "$P(|r|>2)$", "$P(|r|>3)$"
)

footnote_text <- glue(
  "Residual summaries are computed on standardized robust residuals $r = e/\\text{{scale}}$. ",
  "Skewness and kurtosis are sample moment summaries (kurtosis is not excess kurtosis). ",
  "$P(|r|>2)$ and $P(|r|>3)$ quantify tail heaviness relative to a standard normal reference."
)




Code
# compute residual shape summary across all models
purrr::imap_dfr(lmrob_fits, ~ {
  out <- residual_shape(.x)
  out$Model <- gsub("fit_", "", .y)
  out
}) %>%
  dplyr::select(Model, dplyr::everything()) %>%
  as_kable(digits = 3) %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

Code
# create combined Q-Q plots using standardized robust residuals
combined_qq_plot <- function(models, labels, jitter = TRUE) {
  shapes <- (seq_along(models) - 1) %% 25

  plot_data <- purrr::map2_df(
    models, labels,
    ~ data.frame(std_resid = std_res(.x), Model = .y)
  )

  # preserve the supplied label order in the legend (default factor levels
  # sort alphabetically: 1, 10, 11, 2, ...)
  plot_data$Model <- factor(plot_data$Model, levels = unique(unlist(labels)))

  ggplot2::ggplot(plot_data, ggplot2::aes(
    sample = std_resid,
    colour = Model,
    shape = Model
  )) +
    ggplot2::stat_qq(
      size = 2,
      alpha = 0.8,
      position = if (jitter) ggplot2::position_jitter(width = 0.1) else ggplot2::position_identity()
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_abline(
      intercept = 0,
      slope = 1,
      linetype = "dashed",
      linewidth = 0.5,
      color = "black"
    ) +
    viridis::scale_color_viridis(
      discrete = TRUE,
      option = "D",
      begin = 0.1,
      end = 0.9,
      name = "Model"
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = shapes, name = "Model") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Theoretical Quantiles",
      y = "Standardized Robust Residuals"
    ) +
    ggplot2::guides(
      colour = ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
      shape = ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE)
    )
}

combined_qq_plot(lmrob_fits, as.list(as.character(seq_along(lmrob_fits))))

Code
# create scale-location plot for a single robust model
scale_location_plot <- function(fit, name = NULL) {
  point_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[2]
  line_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[4]

  if (!"scale" %in% names(fit)) {
    stop("Model does not contain 'scale' component.")
  }

  std_resid <- stats::residuals(fit) / fit$scale

  model_data <- tibble::tibble(
    .fitted = stats::fitted(fit),
    sqrt_abs_std_resid = sqrt(abs(std_resid))
  )

  ggplot2::ggplot(model_data, ggplot2::aes(.fitted, sqrt_abs_std_resid)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = point_color) +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      formula = y ~ x,
      color = line_color,
      method.args = list(
        span = 1,
        degree = 1,
        family = "symmetric",
        control = stats::loess.control(surface = "direct")
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = name,
      x = "Fitted values",
      y = expression(sqrt("| Std. Residuals |"))
    )
}



Code
purrr::iwalk(lmrob_fits, function(fit, nm) {
  plot(scale_location_plot(fit, name = paste0("Model ", gsub("fit_", "", nm))))
})















































Scale-Location Plots (Robust Models)





1.3 Testing Mean-Structure Adequacy


Code
# marginal model plot: outcome vs linear predictor (fitted values);
# under a correct mean structure the data loess tracks the y = x line
response_predictor_plot <- function(fit, name = NULL) {
  point_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[2]
  line_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[4]

  mf <- stats::model.frame(fit)
  if (ncol(mf) < 2) return(NULL)

  d <- tibble::tibble(
    fitted = stats::fitted(fit),
    y = mf[[1]]
  )

  ggplot2::ggplot(d, ggplot2::aes(fitted, y)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = point_color) +
    ggplot2::geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      method = "loess", se = TRUE, formula = y ~ x, color = line_color,
      method.args = list(span = 1, degree = 1, family = "symmetric",
        control = stats::loess.control(surface = "direct"))
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = name,
      x = "Linear predictor (fitted)",
      y = names(mf)[1]
    )
}



Code
purrr::iwalk(lmrob_fits, function(fit, nm) {
  p <- response_predictor_plot(fit, name = paste0("Model ", gsub("fit_", "", nm)))
  if (!is.null(p)) plot(p)
})















































Outcome vs Linear Predictor (Marginal Model Plots)

# create residuals vs fitted plot for a single robust model
residuals_fitted_plot <- function(fit, name = NULL) {
  point_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[2]
  line_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[4]

  std_resid <- stats::residuals(fit) / fit$scale

  model_data <- tibble::tibble(
    .fitted = stats::fitted(fit),
    std_resid = std_resid
  )

  ggplot2::ggplot(model_data, ggplot2::aes(.fitted, std_resid)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = point_color) +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      formula = y ~ x,
      color = line_color,
      method.args = list(
        span = 1,
        degree = 1,
        family = "symmetric",
        control = stats::loess.control(surface = "direct")
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = name,
      x = "Fitted values",
      y = "Standardized Residuals"
    )
}

  test_res <- lmtest::dwtest(.x)

  data.frame(
    Model = gsub("fit_", "", .y),

    p_value = format_p_value(
      test_res$p.value,
      add_stars = TRUE,

1.5 Assessing Multicollinearity


Code
# compute VIF from model matrix
calc_vif <- function(mm) {
  int <- match("(Intercept)", colnames(mm), nomatch = 0L)
  if (int > 0L) {
    mm <- mm[, -int, drop = FALSE]
  }

  if (ncol(mm) < 2L) {
    return(setNames(numeric(0), character(0)))
  }

  ok <- rowSums(!is.finite(mm)) == 0L
  mm <- mm[ok, , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(mm) < 3L) {
    out <- rep(NA_real_, ncol(mm))
    names(out) <- colnames(mm)
    return(out)
  }

  vifs <- vapply(seq_len(ncol(mm)), function(j) {
    y <- mm[, j]
    x <- mm[, -j, drop = FALSE]

    sst <- sum((y - mean(y))^2)
    if (!is.finite(sst) || sst <= .Machine$double.eps) {
      return(NA_real_)
    }

    aux <- stats::lm.fit(x = cbind(1, x), y = y)
    rss <- sum(aux$residuals^2)
    r2 <- 1 - rss / sst
    r2 <- max(min(r2, 1), 0)

    if (r2 >= 1 - sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)) {
      return(Inf)
    }

    1 / (1 - r2)
  }, numeric(1))

  names(vifs) <- colnames(mm)
  vifs
}

# extract maximum VIF from a fitted model
get_max_vif <- function(fit) {
  mm <- tryCatch(stats::model.matrix(fit), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(mm)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  vifs <- calc_vif(mm)
  if (length(vifs) == 0 || all(is.na(vifs))) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  max(vifs, na.rm = TRUE)
}

1.6 Identifying Influential Observations


Code
# extract participant IDs from fitted model
get_pid <- function(fit) {
  mf <- tryCatch(stats::model.frame(fit), error = function(e) NULL)
  pid <- if (!is.null(mf)) rownames(mf) else NULL

  if (is.null(pid) || anyNA(pid) || any(pid == "")) {
    pid <- as.character(seq_along(stats::residuals(fit)))
  }

  pid
}

# create long-format influence diagnostics for a single model
influence_long_df <- function(fit, model_name) {
  pid <- get_pid(fit)
  n <- length(pid)

  r <- stats::residuals(fit) / fit$scale
  h <- tryCatch(stats::hatvalues(fit), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, n))
  w <- fit$rweights
  if (is.null(w)) {
    w <- rep(NA_real_, n)
  }

  p <- length(stats::coef(fit))
  lev_thr <- 2 * p / n

  score <- abs(r) * (h / pmax(1 - h, .Machine$double.eps))

  tibble::tibble(
    pid = as.character(pid),
    Model = model_name,
    std_resid = as.numeric(r),
    leverage = as.numeric(h),
    rweight = as.numeric(w),
    lev_thr = lev_thr,
    score = as.numeric(score)
  )
}

# map flagged pids back to data rows
match_ids_to_rows <- function(data, pids) {
  pids <- unique(as.character(pids))
  if (length(pids) == 0) {
    return(integer(0))
  }

  candidate_cols <- c("pid", "id", "obs_id", "row_id", "case_id", "participant_id", "idnummer")
  data_cols_lower <- tolower(names(data))

  for (nm in candidate_cols) {
    j <- match(nm, data_cols_lower, nomatch = 0L)
    if (j > 0L) {
      col <- names(data)[j]
      hits <- which(as.character(data[[col]]) %in% pids)
      if (length(hits) > 0) {
        return(hits)
      }
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(rownames(data))) {
    hits <- which(rownames(data) %in% pids)
    if (length(hits) > 0) {
      return(hits)
    }
  }

  idx <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(pids))
  if (!anyNA(idx) && all(idx >= 1) && all(idx <= nrow(data))) {
    return(idx)
  }

  integer(0)
}



in: lmrob_fits (per-model robust diagnostics)
out: influence_results$influential_obs, influence_results$influential_ids
rule: flag observation in a model if robust weight is below the global 5th percentile and \(|r|>2.5\), or if leverage exceeds \(2p/n\) and \(|r|>2.5\); mark as influential if flagged in at least 25% of models or if its maximum score reaches the 99th percentile



Code
# define flagging thresholds
alpha_weight <- 0.05
resid_cut <- 2.5

# compute influence diagnostics across all models
infl_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(lmrob_fits, ~ {
  influence_long_df(.x, model_name = gsub("fit_", "", .y))
})

# global robust-weight threshold (bottom 5% across all models)
w_thr <- stats::quantile(infl_long$rweight, probs = alpha_weight, na.rm = TRUE)

# apply flagging criteria
infl_long <- infl_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_weight = is.finite(rweight) & rweight < w_thr,
    high_leverage = is.finite(leverage) & leverage > lev_thr, # per-model 2p/n column
    large_resid = is.finite(std_resid) & abs(std_resid) > resid_cut,
    flag_model = (low_weight & large_resid) | (high_leverage & large_resid)
  )

k <- length(lmrob_fits)

# aggregate influence flags across models
infl_summary <- infl_long %>%
  dplyr::group_by(pid) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_flag = sum(flag_model, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_abs_resid = max(abs(std_resid), na.rm = TRUE),
    max_leverage = max(leverage, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_rweight = min(rweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_score = max(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# identify influential observations using combined criteria
score_thr <- stats::quantile(infl_summary$max_score, probs = 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)
flag_min_models <- ceiling(k * 0.25)

influential_obs <- infl_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    influential = (n_flag >= flag_min_models) | (max_score >= score_thr)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(influential), dplyr::desc(n_flag), dplyr::desc(max_score))

influential_ids <- influential_obs %>%
  dplyr::filter(influential) %>%
  dplyr::pull(pid) %>%
  unique()

# store results
influence_results <- list(
  influential_obs = influential_obs,
  influential_ids = influential_ids,
  thresholds = list(
    w_thr = w_thr,
    resid_cut = resid_cut,
    lev_rule = "2p/n",
    score_thr = score_thr,
    min_models = flag_min_models
  )
)




Code
# compute summary statistics
num_influential <- length(influence_results$influential_ids)
data_size <- nrow(unadjusted_data)
pct_influential <- if (data_size > 0) round(100 * num_influential / data_size, 2) else NA_real_

htmltools::HTML(glue(
  "<p>Out of {data_size} observations, {num_influential} ({pct_influential}%) were flagged as potentially influential.<br>",
  "Global robust-weight threshold: {round(influence_results$thresholds$w_thr, 3)} (alpha = {alpha_weight}). ",
  "Residual cut: |r| > {resid_cut}. ",
  "Leverage rule: {influence_results$thresholds$lev_rule}. ",
  "Across-model flag: at least {influence_results$thresholds$min_models} of {k} models or max score above the 99th percentile.",
  "</p>"
))

Note:   Rows are aggregated across models. ‘n_flag’ is the number of models where the observation meets the within-model rule (low weight and large residual, or high leverage and large residual). ‘max score’ is max(|r| × h/(1-h)) across models and is used as an additional screen.















Code
# create influence diagnostic panel for a single model
plot_influence_panel <- function(df, flagged_ids, name = NULL) {
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      flagged = pid %in% flagged_ids,
      label = ifelse(flagged, pid, NA_character_),
      influence = 2 / pmax(1 - leverage, .Machine$double.eps)
    )

  breaks <- unique(stats::quantile(df$influence, c(0, 0.33, 0.67, 1), na.rm = TRUE))
  breaks <- breaks[is.finite(breaks)]
  n_intervals <- length(breaks) - 1

  if (n_intervals <= 0) {
    df$influence_cat <- factor("Low", levels = "Low")
  } else {
    labels <- c("Low", "Medium", "High")[seq_len(n_intervals)]
    df$influence_cat <- cut(
      df$influence,
      breaks = breaks,
      labels = labels,
      include.lowest = TRUE
    )
  }

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(leverage, std_resid, color = influence_cat)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      alpha = 0.3,
      formula = y ~ x,
      method.args = list(
        span = 1,
        degree = 1,
        family = "symmetric",
        control = stats::loess.control(surface = "direct")
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = name,
      x = "Leverage",
      y = "Standardized Residuals",
      color = "Influence"
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_viridis_d(option = "D", direction = -1) +
    ggplot2::guides(color = ggplot2::guide_legend(title = "Influence"))

  if (requireNamespace("ggrepel", quietly = TRUE) && any(df$flagged, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    p <- p + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      ggplot2::aes(label = label),
      size = 2,
      max.overlaps = 20,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  }

  p
}

5 Fit All Models


Code
# reuse cached fits when available, otherwise refit the full nested set
if (!recompute_heavy && file.exists(consolidated_path)) {
  message("cached fits found; skipping model fitting (set recompute_heavy <- TRUE to refit)")
} else {
  # fit all models using purrr::imap for named iteration
  all_fits <- purrr::imap(model_specs, function(model_formula, model_name) {
    message(glue::glue("\n{strrep('=', 60)}\nFitting {model_name}...\n"))

    fit_result <- robust_analysis_bootstrap(
      data      = adjusted_data,
      form      = model_formula,
      n_boots   = bootstrap_params$n_boots,
      pct       = bootstrap_params$pct,
      alpha     = bootstrap_params$alpha,
      delta     = bootstrap_params$delta,
      ctrl      = NULL,
      seed      = bootstrap_params$seed,
      psi       = robust_params$psi,
      std_data  = robust_params$std_data
    )

    # save individual fit
    fit_filename <- glue::glue("{type}_fit_{gsub('model_', '', model_name)}.rds")
    saveRDS(fit_result, file.path(models_dir, fit_filename))

    fit_result
  })

  # assign to individual objects for compatibility with downstream steps
  # vectorised, no loop: rename model_* -> fit_* and bind into the chunk environment
  list2env(setNames(all_fits, sub("^model", "fit", names(all_fits))), environment())
}

title: Initial Model Evaluation
text:
3 Model Fit Statistics


Code
# extract goodness-of-fit metrics from model object
get_metrics <- function(model_id, adjusted, robust, fits = model_fits) {
  lev_type <- ifelse(adjusted, "adjusted", "unadjusted")
  est_method <- ifelse(robust, "robust", "ols")
  model_key <- paste0("model_", model_id)

  model_fit <- fits[[lev_type]][[est_method]][[model_key]]$fit

  if (!inherits(model_fit, c("lmrob", "lm"))) {
    stop("Unsupported model class: ", class(model_fit)[1])
  }

  fit_sum <- summary(model_fit)
  n_obs <- length(fit_sum$residuals)
  n_params <- length(coef(model_fit)) + 1

  # compute information criteria (built-in for lm, approximated for lmrob)
  if (inherits(model_fit, "lm") && !inherits(model_fit, "lmrob")) {
    log_lik <- logLik(model_fit)
    aic <- AIC(model_fit)
    bic <- BIC(model_fit)
    rse_lbl <- "Residual_Std_Error"
  } else {
    # approximate log-likelihood for robust models assuming normal errors
    log_lik <- -n_obs / 2 * (log(2 * pi) + 2 * log(fit_sum$sigma) + 1)
    aic <- -2 * log_lik + 2 * n_params
    bic <- -2 * log_lik + log(n_obs) * n_params
    rse_lbl <- "Robust_RSE"
  }

  metrics <- data.frame(
    R_squared = fit_sum$r.squared,
    Adj_R_squared = fit_sum$adj.r.squared,
    RSE = fit_sum$sigma,
    AIC = aic,
    BIC = bic
  )
  colnames(metrics)[3] <- rse_lbl

  metrics
}




Code
# load cached metrics when available, otherwise compute the full grid
if (!recompute_heavy && file.exists(fit_metrics_path)) {
  metrics_df <- readRDS(fit_metrics_path)
} else {
  # create parameter grid for all model × leverage × method combinations
  params <- tidyr::expand_grid(
    model = model_ids,
    adjusted = c(TRUE, FALSE),
    robust = c(TRUE, FALSE)
  )

  # compute metrics for each combination
  metrics_df <- purrr::pmap_dfr(params, function(model, adjusted, robust) {
    met <- get_metrics(model, adjusted, robust)
    data.frame(
      model = model,
      leverage = ifelse(adjusted, "Adjusted", "Unadjusted"),
      method = ifelse(robust, "RLM", "OLS"),
      R_squared = met$R_squared,
      Adj_R_squared = met$Adj_R_squared,
      RSE = met[, 3],
      AIC = met$AIC,
      BIC = met$BIC
    )
  }) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(model, leverage) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      leverage = dplyr::if_else(dplyr::row_number() == 1,
        stringr::str_to_title(leverage), NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # suppress R² for intercept-only model (model 0) as it is undefined
  r2_cols <- grepl("R_squared", names(metrics_df))
  metrics_df[metrics_df$model == 0, r2_cols] <- NA

  saveRDS(metrics_df, fit_metrics_path)
}

# create row labels for table grouping
model_rows <- paste("Model", metrics_df$model)




Code
# table column names and footnote
col_names_fits <- c(
  "Leverage", "Method", "$R^2$", "$R^2 (adj.)$",
  "Std. Error (RSE / Robust RSE)", "AIC", "BIC"
)

footnote_text_fits <- glue(
  "'Leverage' indicates whether influential observations were removed (Adjusted vs. Unadjusted).",
  "'Method' specifies estimation approach (OLS or RLM).",
  "$R^2$ denotes coefficient of determination;",
  "$R^2 (adj.)$ is its adjusted variant. 'Std. Error'",
  "reports residual standard error (OLS) or",
  "robust residual scale estimate (RLM).",
  "AIC and BIC are Akaike and Bayesian information criteria. ",
  "Higher $R^2$ indicates better fit;",
  "lower AIC/BIC suggests better model performance. ",
  "RLM AIC/BIC are normal-likelihood approximations and are not directly comparable to OLS values."
)

metrics_df %>%
  dplyr::select(-model) %>%
  as_kable(sci_threshold = FALSE, omit_leading_zero = TRUE, digits = 3) %>%
  kableExtra::pack_rows(index = row_grouping(model_rows), escape = FALSE) %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

title: Initial Model Evaluation
text:
4 Nested Model Comparisons


Code
# compare two nested models using Wald and deviance tests
compare_models <- function(mod1, mod2) {
  extract_p_value <- function(x) {
    pr_cols <- grep("^Pr", names(x), value = TRUE)
    if (length(pr_cols) == 0) {
      return(NA_real_)
    }
    tail(x[[pr_cols[1]]], 1)
  }

  tryCatch(
    {
      if (inherits(mod1, "lmrob")) {
        wald <- anova(mod1, mod2, test = "Wald")
        dev <- anova(mod1, mod2, test = "Deviance")

        data.frame(
          df1 = mod1$degree.freedom,
          df2 = mod2$degree.freedom,
          wald_stat = tail(wald$Test.Stat, 1),
          wald_p = extract_p_value(wald),
          deviance_stat = tail(dev$Test.Stat, 1),
          deviance_p = extract_p_value(dev),
          df = tail(wald$Df, 1)
        )
      } else {
        wald <- lmtest::waldtest(mod1, mod2, test = "Chisq")
        aov <- anova(mod1, mod2)

        data.frame(
          df1 = mod1$df.residual,
          df2 = mod2$df.residual,
          wald_stat = wald$Chisq[2],
          wald_p = extract_p_value(wald),
          deviance_stat = aov$F[2],
          deviance_p = extract_p_value(aov),
          df = aov$Df[2]
        )
      }
    },
    error = function(e) {
      warning("Model comparison failed: ", e$message)
      data.frame(
        df1 = NA, df2 = NA, wald_stat = NA, wald_p = NA,
        deviance_stat = NA, deviance_p = NA, df = NA
      )
    }
  )
}




Code
# generate nested model comparison table
compare_nested_models <- function(fits, leverage, method,
                                  col_names = NULL, footnote_text = NULL) {
  models <- purrr::map(fits[[leverage]][[method]], ~ .x$fit)

  mn <- names(models)
  model_nums <- as.integer(sub(".*_", "", mn))

  ord <- order(model_nums)
  models <- models[ord]
  mn <- mn[ord]
  model_nums <- model_nums[ord]

  is_nested <- function(mod1, mod2) {
    terms1 <- attr(terms(mod1), "term.labels")
    terms2 <- attr(terms(mod2), "term.labels")
    all(terms1 %in% terms2) && !setequal(terms1, terms2)
  }

  num_combs <- combn(model_nums, 2, simplify = FALSE)
  name_combs <- combn(mn, 2, simplify = FALSE)

  nested_idx <- purrr::map_lgl(name_combs, function(nv) {
    is_nested(models[[nv[1]]], models[[nv[2]]])
  })

  num_combs <- num_combs[nested_idx]
  name_combs <- name_combs[nested_idx]

  model_pairs <- Map(
    function(nums, nv) setNames(nums, nv),
    num_combs, name_combs
  )

  comps <- model_pairs %>%
    purrr::map_df(~ {
      mod1 <- models[[names(.x)[1]]]
      mod2 <- models[[names(.x)[2]]]
      data.frame(
        model_a = .x[1],
        model_b = .x[2],
        model_pair = sprintf("%d vs. %d", .x[1], .x[2]),
        suppress_output(compare_models(mod1, mod2))
      )
    })

  labs <- glue("Model {comps$model_a}")

  comps %>%
    dplyr::rowwise() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      wald_stat = format_coef(wald_stat, wald_p),
      deviance_stat = format_coef(deviance_stat, deviance_p)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(-model_a, -model_b, -wald_p, -deviance_p) %>%
    as_kable() %>%
    kableExtra::pack_rows(index = row_grouping(labs), escape = FALSE) %>%
    footnote() %>%
    add_style()
}



4.1 Robust Linear Models (RLM)


Code
# column names and footnote for RLM nested comparisons
col_names <- c(
  "$\\text{}$",
  "$DF_{\\text{pseudo1}}$",
  "$DF_{\\text{pseudo2}}$",
  "Wald $\\chi^2$",
  "Deviance",
  "DF"
)

footnote_text <- glue(
  "{pval_str(use_dot_for_0.1 = TRUE, html = FALSE)}. ",
  "Pseudo-degrees of freedom ($DF_{{\\text{{pseudo}}}}$)",
  "represent adjusted degrees of freedom in robust models. ",
  "$DF_{{\\text{{pseudo1}}}}$ corresponds to the simpler model;",
  "$DF_{{\\text{{pseudo2}}}}$ to the more complex model. ",
  "Wald and Deviance statistics assess improvements in nested models."
)




Code
if (!recompute_heavy && file.exists(nested_rlm_unadj_path)) {
  nested_rlm_unadjusted <- readRDS(nested_rlm_unadj_path)
} else {
  nested_rlm_unadjusted <- compare_nested_models(model_fits, "unadjusted", "robust")
  saveRDS(nested_rlm_unadjusted, nested_rlm_unadj_path)
}

title: Interaction Effects & Johnson–Neyman Analysis
text:
1 Load Project Resources


Code
# clear environment
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions (consistent with steps 7-9)
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "as_kable", "footnote", "set_globals", "beautify_names", "structure_models",
  "sci_to_tex", "row_grouping", "md_to_html", "pval_str", "format_p_value",
  "dim_ci_rows", "add_style", "format_coef", "import_pipes", "numeric_df",
  "suppress_output", "named_list"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

title: Interaction Effects & Johnson–Neyman Analysis
Interaction Effects & Johnson–Neyman Analysis
Interaction Effects & Johnson–Neyman Analysis
text:
4 JN Results


Code
# attach robust model fits for formula-style access
robust_models <- model_fits$adjusted$robust
if (!"robust_models" %in% search()) {
  attach(robust_models)
}

# extract sample size for footnotes
n_obs <- nrow(model.frame(model_fits$adjusted$robust$model_1$fit))




Code
# column names for JN interval tables
col_names <- c(
  "Predictor", "$\\Delta x$", "Modx Group",
  "$\\overline{\\beta}$", "$\\text{CI}_{\\text{low}}$",
  "$\\text{CI}_{\\text{high}}$", "$\\Sigma \\text{ Sig.}$",
  "$\\Sigma \\text{ Total}$", "$\\% \\text{Sig.}$"
)

# footnote text for JN interval tables
footnote_text <- glue::glue(
  "$N={n_obs}$ (sensitivity sample excluding influential observations). ",
  "For each moderator bin ($\\Delta x$), mean slopes ($\\overline{{\\beta}}$) ",
  "and 95\\% CIs are shown. $\\Sigma$ Sig. counts points within the bin where ",
  "$p \\lt .05$; \\% Sig. = $\\Sigma$ Sig. / $\\Sigma$ Total. ",
  "Greyed rows indicate CIs spanning zero."
)



4.1 Model 4: Gender × SES (Composite)

Code
m4 <- jn_analysis(model_4) %T>%
  plot(plot_significant_only = FALSE) %>%
  summarize_jn_intervals()

  m4 %>%
    dplyr::select(-term2, -model) %>%
    as_kable() %>%
    dim_ci_rows(m4, opacity = 0.25) %>%
    kableExtra::pack_rows(index = row_grouping(m4$term2), escape = FALSE) %>%
    footnote() %>%
    add_style()
} else {
  cat("No JN intervals available for Model 4 (no significant interactions or missing bootstrap estimates).")
}

title: Bootstrapped Validation of Model Performance and Robustness
text:
1 Load Project Resources


Code
# clear environment
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions (consistent with steps 7-11)
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "set_globals", "import_pipes", "numeric_df", "add_style",
  "md_to_html", "rm_dup_cols", "format_coef", "suppress_output",
  "named_list", "sci_to_tex", "as_kable", "pval_str", "footnote",
  "beautify_names", "format_p_value", "structure_models",
  "dim_ci_rows", "row_grouping"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

# global recompute switch
# TRUE re-runs the heavy steps; FALSE loads cached assets when they exist
recompute_heavy <- FALSE

title: Bootstrapped Validation of Model Performance and Robustness
text:
2 Load Data and Models


Code
# define input file paths
adjusted_data_path <- here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "adjusted_data.rds")
unadjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "unadjusted_fits.rds")
adjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "adjusted_fits.rds")

# verify input files exist
required_files <- c(adjusted_data_path, unadjusted_fits_path, adjusted_fits_path)
missing_files <- required_files[!file.exists(required_files)]
if (length(missing_files) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required input files: ", paste(basename(missing_files), collapse = ", "))
}

# load adjusted dataset from step 8
source_data <- readRDS(adjusted_data_path)

# load fits and structure into nested list (unadjusted/adjusted × ols/robust)
unadjusted_fits <- readRDS(unadjusted_fits_path)
adjusted_fits <- readRDS(adjusted_fits_path)
model_fits <- structure_models(unadjusted_fits, adjusted_fits)

# extract robust models from adjusted fits and assign custom class for dispatch
lmrobs <- model_fits$adjusted$robust %>%
  purrr::map(~ `class<-`(.x, c("model", class(.x))))

# extract model ids
model_ids <- names(lmrobs) %>%
  stringr::str_extract("[0-9]+$") %>%
  as.integer() %>%
  sort()




Code
# verification block to confirm step 7/9 alignment
n_models <- length(model_ids)
model_range <- paste0("Models ", min(model_ids), "–", max(model_ids))
n_obs <- nrow(source_data)

verify_msg <- glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
<strong>Step 7/9 Model Verification</strong><br><br>
<strong>Models loaded:</strong> {n_models} models ({model_range})<br>
<strong>Adjusted fits:</strong> From Step 9 (<code>adjusted_fits.rds</code>)<br>
<strong>Analysis dataset:</strong> {n_obs} observations (sensitivity sample from Step 8)<br>
<strong>Model types:</strong> Composite (Models 0–6) and Disaggregated (Models 7–11)
</div>"
)
cat(verify_msg)







Note



Step 7/9 Model Verification Models loaded: 12 models (Models 0–11) Adjusted fits: From Step 9 (adjusted_fits.rds) Analysis dataset: 908 observations (sensitivity sample from Step 8) Model types: Composite (Models 0–6) and Disaggregated (Models 7–11)

title: Bootstrapped Validation of Model Performance and Robustness
Post-Imputation Data Finalization
text:
Code
# clear global env and force full garbage collection
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))

# detach non-base packages to avoid namespace interference
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions for data handling and analysis
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "summarize_na", "set_globals", "describe", "import_pipes",
  "add_style", "beautify_names", "as_kable", "format_p_value",
  "footnote", "md_to_html", "numeric_df"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

# load imputed dataset from step 5
imputed_data <- haven::read_sav(
  here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "imputed_data_combined.sav")
)

text:
3 SES Variable Structure


Code
col_names <- c("Variable", "Present", "$n_{valid}$", "$\\%_{valid}$")
footnote_text <- glue("$N = {n_obs}$")

ses_structure <- data.frame(
  Variable = c(
    "education_self", "education_partner", "education_father",
    "education_mother", "education_parental",
    "isei08_self", "isei08_partner", "isei08_father",
    "isei08_mother", "isei08_parental"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Present = ifelse(Variable %in% names(imputed_data),
      "$\\checkmark$", "$\\times$"
    ),
    n_valid = sapply(Variable, function(v) {
      if (v %in% names(imputed_data)) {
        sum(!is.na(imputed_data[[v]]))
      } else {
        NA_integer_
      }
    }),
    pct_valid = sapply(Variable, function(v) {
      if (v %in% names(imputed_data)) {
        round(100 * mean(!is.na(imputed_data[[v]])), 1)
      } else {
        NA_real_
      }
    })
  )

ses_structure$Present %<>% md_to_html()

  as_kable() %>%
  footnote() %>%

4 Summarize Missing Data Post-Imputation
After imputation, only structural missingness should remain (partner variables for non-partnered respondents).

col_names <- c("Variable", "$n_{miss}$", "$\\%_{miss}$", "Structural$^{1}$")

  "$N = {n_obs}$; ",
  "$^{{1}}$ Structural missingness = partner-specific variables for non-partnered respondents."
)




Code
# summarize missingness for key analysis variables
missing_summary <- imputed_data %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(all_expected)) %>%
  summarize_na() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    variable = beautify_names(variable),
    structural = ifelse(
      grepl("partner", variable, ignore.case = TRUE) & n_miss > 0,
      "Yes", "No"
    )
  )

title: Post-Imputation Data Finalization
text:
8 Variable Summary


Code
col_names <- c(
  "Variable", "Type", "$n_{valid}$", "$n_{miss}$",
  "$\\%_{miss}$", "$n_{unique}$", "Category"
)

footnote_text <- glue("$N = {n_obs}$")




Code
# create comprehensive variable summary
var_summary <- data.frame(
  variable = names(imputed_data),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    type = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) {
      if (is.numeric(x)) "Numeric" else if (is.factor(x)) "Factor" else class(x)[1]
    }),
    n_valid = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) sum(!is.na(x))),
    n_miss = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) sum(is.na(x))),
    pct_miss = round(100 * n_miss / n_obs, 1),
    n_unique = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) length(unique(x[!is.na(x)]))),
    category = dplyr::case_when(
      variable == "idnummer" ~ "Identifier",
      grepl("^education", variable) ~ "Education",
      grepl("^isei08", variable) ~ "Occupation (ISEI-08)",
      grepl("^isco08r", variable) ~ "Occupation (ISCO-08)",
      grepl("^brc14", variable) ~ "Occupation (BRC-14)",
      grepl("^dhd", variable) ~ "Dietary Outcome",
      grepl("income", variable) ~ "Income",
      grepl("partner", variable) ~ "Partner Status",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(category, variable) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(variable = beautify_names(variable))

var_summary %>%
  as_kable() %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

title: Unadjusted Regression Models
text:
4 Disaggregated SES Models (Models 7–11)
These models use separate variables for mother, father, and partner education and occupation.

4.1 Model 7: Mother and Father SES Main Effects (H1, RQ1)


Code
model_7 <- update(model_1, . ~ . +
  education_mother +
  education_father +
  isei08_mother +
  isei08_father)




4.2 Model 8: Add Partner SES Main Effects (H2, RQ2)


Code
model_8 <- update(model_7, . ~ . +
  education_partner:partner +
  isei08_partner:partner)




4.3 Model 9: Gender × Mother/Father/Partner SES Interactions (H3, H4, RQ3)


Code
model_9 <- update(model_8, . ~ . +
  woman:education_mother +
  woman:education_father +
  woman:isei08_mother +
  woman:isei08_father +
  woman:education_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_partner:partner)




4.4 Model 10: Mother × Partner and Father × Partner Synergy (H5, RQ4)


Code
model_10 <- update(model_9, . ~ . +
  education_mother:education_partner:partner +
  education_father:education_partner:partner +
  isei08_mother:isei08_partner:partner +
  isei08_father:isei08_partner:partner)




4.5 Model 11: Gender × Parent-Specific Synergy (H6, RQ4)


Code
model_11 <- update(model_10, . ~ . +
  woman:education_mother:education_partner:partner +
  woman:education_father:education_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_mother:isei08_partner:partner +
  woman:isei08_father:isei08_partner:partner)
